title: Elettaria ensal
intro:
There is no information available on the plant Elettaria ensal. However, Elettaria cardamomum is a known plant species. If you meant Elettaria cardamomum, here's a paragraph about it:

Elettaria cardamomum, commonly known as cardamom, is a perennial herb native to India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its health benefits, including digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Elettaria cardamomum include volatile oils, such as limonene, linalool, and alpha-terpineol, as well as flavonoids and glycosides. In traditional medicine, Elettaria cardamomum is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, capsules, and powders, which are often used to promote digestion, relieve nausea, and freshen breath.
taxonomy: Elettaria cardamomum and Elettaria ensal are sometimes confused with each other due to the similarity in their names.  Elettaria ensal is not a widely recognized or documented species. constituents: Elettaria cardamomum (not Elettaria ensal) is the correct name for the plant in question, however I'll proceed to answer your question based on Elettaria cardamomum. Elettaria cardamomum contains a variety of active constituents, including cardamom oil, cardamom lignans, and a range of other compounds such as limonene, linalool, and alpha-terpineol. These constituents are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
